name: Shared Scan Service
description: Shared scan execution, persisted findings, and ack/exclude state for dead-code, duplicate detection, type-escape detection, and hybrid code search, centralized in @code-workbench/mcp-core. The VS Code extension host runs the scans and persists results; MCP tools read those results and apply current acks at read time.
guidelines: scan-runner owns spawning logic + groupFingerprint; callers supply nodeBin + scriptPath
scan-runner spawns four detectors: dead-code-detect, clone-detect, type-escape-detect (runTypeEscapeScan), and code-search (runCodeSearch)
groupFingerprint hashes members' normHash (normalized-token content), not line positions — acks survive edits; file:name is the legacy fallback only
scan-state owns ack/exclude JSON r/w keyed by ScanFeature ('dead-code'|'duplicates'|'type-escapes')

findings-store owns the persisted-findings r/w keyed by the same ScanFeature: <repo>/.code-workbench/<feature>-findings.json. Schema v1 = { schemaVersion, generatedAt, root, items|groups }. Atomic write via tmp+rename.
Host-writes / MCP-reads contract: only the host (VS Code extension) calls runDeadCodeScan/runDuplicateScan/runTypeEscapeScan with persistTo: repoPath. MCP servers read the file with readFindings.
Findings are stored UNFILTERED by acks — acks are applied at read time so toggling an ack does not invalidate the persisted file
Host-specific path resolution stays with the caller (the extension), not in shared modules
Extension scan panels extend the generic ScanViewProvider<T>; per-feature views only supply a webview script + ScanViewConfig
scan-types is type-only: dual .d.ts/.d.cts declarations, no runtime export
anti_patterns: Do not inline execFile/readJsonArray/writeJsonArray in host code — use scan-runner/scan-state/findings-store
Do not define groupFingerprint in multiple places — it lives in scan-runner.cjs
Do not make a fingerprint depend on line numbers or OS path separators — acks then break on edits or diverge between surfaces
Do not add Electron or vscode imports to mcp-core modules
Do not reimplement the scan/ack/error-handling loop per view — use ScanViewProvider
Do not write acks into the findings file — acks are applied at read time so toggling them never invalidates findings
Do not make MCP tools call runDeadCodeScan/runDuplicateScan/runTypeEscapeScan inline by default — they should read the host-written findings file; force_scan: true is the explicit escape hatch
decisions: CJS implementation + MJS shim pattern (same as task-store) so CJS extension host and ESM MCP servers share one copy
groupFingerprint centralized here (previously duplicated across ast-server.mjs and host code) to eliminate the duplication
ScanViewProvider<T> is the single WebviewView implementation; DeadCode/Duplicates/TypeEscape views are thin subclasses
code-search.mjs is bundled with @xenova/transformers external — the extension's code search degrades to BM25-only since the optional model dep is not shipped in the .vsix

Findings persistence introduced so MCP tool calls are O(read JSON) instead of triggering full scans. UI Rescan is the canonical scan trigger; MCP tools expose force_scan: true as an escape hatch for headless workflows.
Findings stored unfiltered + acks applied at read time, so toggling an ack never invalidates the persisted scan.
type-escapes added as a third scan feature (2026-05); see [[type-escape-detection]]. Categories ARE the kinds (no remap), and TypeEscapeItem carries an extra content snippet field.
